creator: grahamperrin@gmail.com
text:
Re: the Stack Exchange answer in comment 4

> Prefix it with "hash:" in the wpa_supplicant.conf file, i.e.
> 
> password=hash:6602f435f01b9173889a8d3b9bdcfd0b

After changing my /etc/wpa_supplicant.conf to use 
hash:⋯
instead of clear text, I lost the ability to connect to eduroam. 

Reverted to clear text. 

I assume that the change to hash:⋯ (alone) is insufficient for a working configuration. 

Discussions may be complicated and protracted, I'll continue elsewhere (keep noise in Bugzilla to a minimum) and return here if there's a conclusion. 

I have: 

- two threads in the wifi-hacking channel in FreeBSD Discord 
  <https://discord.com/channels/727023752348434432/1324131132161134645>

- threads in the email lists that were mentioned in comment 2, however 
  that was no more than two lists (netiquette) and neither list was 
  freebsd-wireless, so I might recommence in freebsd-wireless.

(In reply to Mark Peek from comment #6)

Thank you! Very useful, it should be added to FreeBSD Project documentation.
